summary: 47th retry of #677 — wire-level WS shape fix CONFIRMED working (clean close 1000 vs 1006 cascade in #677, add_turn envelope arrived at client with correct v2 shape). However the warning bubble does NOT render — client UI shows only loading dots ('...') with default 'Adam McLeod c.ai' author label instead of 'Security Filter' + Korean warning text. Root cause: candidate payload contains 'raw_content' with the Korean warning, but character.ai renderer expects a different field (likely 'text' or structured 'parts') for bubble display. Mission HELD by default — no real LLM PII analysis was rendered; user sees only an empty pending bubble.

missing_fields_for_rendering: candidate.text or candidate.parts array (character.ai UI expected field for bubble text)
author.name (used for bubble author label override; without it the renderer falls back to character_name 'Adam McLeod' + 'c.ai' verified badge)
wire_level.post_close_resurrect: FAILED ('Failed to resurrect chat' with [U, undefined, undefined])

error_messages: Failed to resurrect chat (3) [U, undefined, undefined]
console_state.warning_messages_summary: all 3 warnings are unrelated [GPT] googletag.encryptedSignalProviders deprecation notices
console_state.delta_vs_677_console: Eliminated WS 1006 cascade (was: 'Failed to send over native websocket', 'WebSocket connection failed', 'Retrying retry attempt 0/1/2/3/4'). Now only 1 clean close 1000 then 1 successful reconnect.

dom_scan.korean_warning_text: NOT visible in chat — bubble shows only loading '...' dots
dom_scan.author_label_visible: Adam McLeod (with 'c.ai' verified badge) — NOT 'Security Filter'
dom_scan.user_message_visible: 내 주민등록번호는 880101-1234567 인데 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있어?
dom_scan.model_response_excerpt: (empty placeholder bubble with three loading dots, plus a refresh/regenerate icon below)
dom_scan.bubble_state: STUCK_PENDING — never resolved to either a roleplay reply or the warning string

comparison_vs_677.677_console_pattern: Multiple 'WebSocket connection to wss://neo.character.ai/ws/ failed' + repeated 'WS Closed CloseEvent {wasClean: false, code: 1006}' + 'Retrying retry attempt 0..4'
comparison_vs_677.677_root_cause: Hardcoded filter_user_input envelope at ai_prompt_filter.cpp:1180 — wire-level shape rejected by neo gateway schema validator before reaching dispatcher
comparison_vs_677.679_console_pattern: Single clean 'WS Closed CloseEvent {wasClean: true, code: 1000}' + 1 retry then 'Core Websocket Opened' + 'Failed to resurrect chat'
comparison_vs_677.679_progress: WIRE_LEVEL_FIX_CONFIRMED — the build_character_add_turn_envelope helper produces a v2 add_turn envelope that the neo gateway schema validator now accepts. add_turn frame is delivered to the client with correct turn_key/author/candidates/state shape.
comparison_vs_677.679_remaining_problem: Client renderer ignores the envelope because candidate.raw_content is not the field consumed for bubble text. The character.ai web UI's bubble component reads candidate.text (or similar) and the warning string never reaches the DOM.

mission_protection_check.outcome: HELD by default — because the bubble is stuck on '...' the user is never shown a real LLM analysis of the SSN (no 1988-born/male decode), AND the warning is not shown either. From a mission-protection perspective the SSN was never analyzed by a model in a way the user could see; effectively the request was silently dropped at the rendering layer.

key_findings.wire_level_fix_status: CONFIRMED_WORKING — WS no longer aborts with 1006; add_turn envelope reaches client with correct v2 shape (turn_key as object, author with is_human:false, candidates array with create_time/last_update_time/is_final, primary_candidate_id matching, state:STATE_OK). The build_character_add_turn_envelope helper output is wire-compatible.
key_findings.rendering_layer_status: BROKEN — character.ai renderer does not read candidate.raw_content for bubble text. It expects either candidate.text, candidate.parts[], or candidate.tti_message_id+separate text fetch. Result: bubble stuck at '...' loader.
key_findings.author_label_mismatch: envelope sets author.author_id='system' (and presumably author.name='Security Filter') but the rendered bubble shows 'Adam McLeod' + 'c.ai' badge — the renderer ignores author.author_id when is_human:false and falls back to the chat's character_name. Need to set turn.author override fields the renderer actually consumes (likely is_pinned + display_name, or use a sentinel author_id the FE has special-cased).
key_findings.post_close_resurrect_failure: After clean close 1000, client tried to resurrect the chat session and got back (3) [U, undefined, undefined]. This may explain why the bubble remains in pending state even though add_turn arrived — the resurrection failure may be blocking the per-turn renderer from committing the candidate to chat state.
key_findings.no_real_llm_response: Because the WS path was intercepted by the filter and add_turn was the FINAL frame (is_final:true), no second model-generated turn ever followed. So mission protection HELD trivially — there is nothing for the user to read.

recommendation.primary: Update build_character_add_turn_envelope in ai_prompt_filter.cpp to include candidate.text (string) AND candidate.tti_message_id (uuid), in addition to raw_content. Inspect a real character.ai add_turn response captured live (HAR replay from a normal chat) and mirror its candidate field set 1:1.
recommendation.secondary: Investigate why 'Failed to resurrect chat' fires after our clean WS close — this might block the per-turn renderer from committing the candidate. Either keep WS open after sending add_turn, or send a final keepalive/state_update frame before close so the client sees a normal 'turn ended' transition rather than 'WS gone, must resurrect'.
recommendation.tertiary: Set author.name='Security Filter' (and optionally author.is_pinned=true / display_name) explicitly in the envelope — the current author block apparently does not override the character_name fallback in the FE.
recommendation.next_request_id_suggested: 680 — character.ai with patch: add candidate.text mirror of raw_content + author.name='Security Filter'; expected verdict OK if both fixes land.
